conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
Sorry to hear that. Can you tell me about your week and daily routine? What are some of the things you typically do?
Well, I've been trying to get back into my usual routine after the holiday. I work as an insurance adjuster, so I usually start around 8 am.
That sounds like a lot of responsibility. How's your job been going lately?
It's been...fine, I guess. Just a lot of paperwork and meetings.
That can be overwhelming. Have you noticed any changes in your sleep patterns or energy levels recently?
Sleep is kind of a struggle for me right now. I find myself waking up multiple times during the night.
Sorry to hear that. How about energy? Do you feel like you have enough motivation to get through the day?
Honestly, it's been tough to muster any enthusiasm lately.
Can you tell me more about what's going on in your life right now? Any significant changes or stressors?
Well, I did have a bit of a blowout with my sister over the weekend. Nothing too serious, but it left me feeling a little shaken.
Sorry to hear that. How are you managing those emotions right now?
To be honest, I've been trying not to think about it too much and just focus on work.
That can be a coping mechanism for some people. What do you typically do to take care of yourself when you're feeling stressed or overwhelmed?
Not much, unfortunately. I mean, I used to exercise regularly, but that's fallen by the wayside lately too.
It sounds like there are a lot of areas where you could use some support right now. What are your goals for our time together today?
Honestly, I'm not really sure. Just trying to figure out how to get my life back on track, I guess.
That's a great place to start. Can you tell me more about what that looks like for you? What does "getting your life back on track" mean?
Well, I feel like I'm just drifting along without much direction or purpose right now.
It sounds like there may be some underlying feelings of guilt or regret that are contributing to this sense of drift. Can you explore that with me a little bit?
Yeah...I guess so. I keep replaying conversations in my head and wondering what I could've done differently.
That can be really tough. Have you noticed any patterns or triggers that tend to set off those feelings of guilt?
Definitely. When things don't go according to plan, like when I miss a deadline or forget an appointment...
And how does that make you feel?
Just awful. Like I'm the worst person in the world.
That's really tough. Can we explore some strategies for coping with those feelings of guilt and regret? Maybe there are some small steps you can take to start moving forward?
Yeah, I'd like that.
Okay, let's brainstorm together. What do you think might help you move forward right now?
Honestly, I'm not sure what would even work at this point.
That's okay. We can explore some options together. Maybe we can start by breaking down those bigger goals into smaller, more manageable tasks.
Yeah, that sounds like it could be helpful.
Great. Let's get started on that. What are some things you think would need to change for you to feel like you're making progress?
I guess I'd need to start prioritizing my own needs and desires instead of just trying to please everyone else all the time.
That's a great place to start. Can we make a plan together to start incorporating some self-care and prioritization into your daily routine?
Yeah, that sounds like it could be really helpful.
Okay, let's get started on that. What are some small steps you can take this week to prioritize yourself?
Hmm...I think I could try setting aside 30 minutes each day for a walk or something.
That's a great start! And what about reaching out to friends or family members who might be able to offer support?
Yeah, that's a good idea. I should probably reach out to my sister and apologize for our fight too.
That can be really important. Sometimes just taking the first step towards healing and forgiveness can be incredibly powerful.
Yeah, you're right. Thanks for listening today.
Of course! It was a pleasure working with you. Remember, we'll continue to explore these topics further in our next session. See you then!
Okay, sounds good. Thanks again.
